about_description: Revolutionizing digital addressing in Africa through inclusive technology, community validation and precise georeferencing.
mission_text: Provide every African citizen with a unique, verifiable and permanent digital address, promoting financial inclusion, access to essential services and strengthening continental identity.
vision_text: Become the reference continental digital addressing system, connecting over 1 billion Africans and serving as fundamental infrastructure for the sustainable development of the continent.
values_subtitle: Fundamental principles that guide every decision and action
value_inclusion_desc: We believe every African deserves a verifiable digital address, regardless of their location or socioeconomic situation.
value_security_desc: We protect user identity and privacy through community validation and advanced encryption.
value_community_desc: The system is validated by the community itself, ensuring authenticity and strengthening social bonds.
value_interoperability_desc: We build a continental system that works beyond borders, connecting all of Africa.
team_subtitle: Experts dedicated to revolutionizing digital addressing in Africa
cta_about_description: Be part of the movement transforming digital addressing in Africa
timeline_conception_desc: Start of continental digital addressing concept development for Africa
timeline_prototyping_desc: Development of first prototype and testing with communities in Angola
timeline_community_validation_desc: Implementation of witness validation system and authorization levels
timeline_regional_expansion_desc: Launch in multiple African countries with multilingual support
timeline_financial_integration_desc: Partnerships with financial institutions for banking inclusion
timeline_public_api_desc: Launch of open API for developers and businesses
team_ceo_desc: PhD in Geographic Information Systems, 15 years of experience in African digital infrastructure.
team_cto_desc: Former Google Maps lead engineer, specialist in georeferencing and distributed systems.
team_community_desc: Led digital inclusion projects in 12 African countries, fluent in 6 African languages.
team_engineering_desc: Systems architect with experience in large-scale platforms and blockchain.

faq_subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about AFROLOC
try_different_search: Try searching with different terms or adjust filters
contact_support_text: Our team is ready to help you
faq_a1: AFROLOC is a continental digital addressing system that provides each African citizen with a unique, verifiable identifier based on their location. The system uses GPS georeferencing, community validation, and multiple authorization levels to create a permanent, reliable digital address.
faq_a2: AFROLOC works in three main steps: 1) You create your digital address by providing your exact GPS location; 2) Community members validate your presence at the location through the app; 3) Regional authorities can add extra validation. Your AFROLOC is then generated as a unique code that can be used to receive mail, access financial services, and more.
faq_a3: Yes! Creating your first personal AFROLOC is completely free. Validators can create up to 5 IDs for free. Only companies that need advanced features, full API, or unlimited IDs need paid plans.
faq_q4: In which countries is AFROLOC available?
faq_a4: AFROLOC is currently available in over 12 African countries, including Angola, Mozambique, Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, South Africa, Senegal, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, DR Congo, Tanzania, and Uganda. We are constantly expanding to new countries.
faq_q5: Do I need internet to use AFROLOC?
faq_a5: To create an AFROLOC you need internet. However, we offer offline mode that allows you to create identities without connection and sync later when you have internet access. You can also view your existing IDs offline.

hero_title: A digital address for everyone — even without a street or number.
hero_description: Millions of people in Africa have no address — and without one, there's no banking, deliveries or services. Create your AFROLOC: a unique, verifiable code for where you live.
why_description: Hundreds of millions of Africans — possibly over 500 million — do not have a formal address
feature_addressing_desc: Direct georeferencing even in areas without named streets
feature_validation_desc: System of neighboring witnesses and local authorities
feature_financial_desc: Access to banking services, credit and insurance
feature_verification_desc: Automatic review every 6-18 months depending on zone
feature_interoperability_desc: Compatible between countries and administrative structures
feature_apis_desc: Easy integration with fintechs, postal services and e-government
step1_desc: Mark your residence with GPS coordinates via mobile app. The system generates a provisional Afroloc based on your location and administrative structure.
step2_desc: Three neighbors and a local authority confirm your residence. Confirmations are made via SMS or digital signature.
step3_desc: Automatic renewal via periodic notifications (6-18 months depending on zone). All changes are recorded with timestamp.
cta_description: Join thousands of people on the African continent with verified digital addresses

challenge_desc: Hundreds of millions of Africans — possibly over 500 million — do not have a formal address
challenge_step1_desc: In Kinshasa, Lagos, Luanda and other African cities, millions live without official addresses
challenge_step2_desc: Ambulances don't arrive, mail gets lost, deliveries are impossible
challenge_step3_desc: African banks require proof of residence that most people don't have
challenge_step4_desc: Developed for and by Africa, respecting our urban realities
addressing_desc: Direct georeferencing even in areas without named streets
addressing_step1_desc: In the outskirts of Luanda or commune of Kinshasa, GPS locates your home
addressing_step2_desc: Adapted to each country's divisions: provinces, municipalities, sectors, neighborhoods
addressing_step4_desc: Works with intermittent internet, common in many African cities
validation_step1_desc: Local administration officer visits blocks and registers residents
validation_step2_desc: Groups 50-100 registrations from the same neighborhood for joint validation
validation_step3_desc: Local authority knows the community and validates authenticity
validation_step4_desc: Escalates to municipality, province, maintaining African chain of trust
financial_desc: Access to banking services, credit and insurance in Africa
financial_step1_desc: BAI, BCI, Ecobank accept Afroloc to open accounts for informal traders
financial_step2_desc: African cooperatives and IMFs can locate small traders
financial_step3_desc: M-Pesa, Orange Money link digital payments to physical address
financial_step4_desc: African insurers offer protection to those who never had access
verification_desc: Update adapted to African urban growth
verification_step1_desc: Luanda, Lagos, Kinshasa grow fast: biannual verification
verification_step2_desc: Stable urban areas: annual update is sufficient
verification_step3_desc: Notification via popular channels in Africa when time to verify
verification_step4_desc: Community agent visits or resident confirms at administrative post
interop_desc: Compatible across 54 African countries and their structures
interop_step1_desc: AU-recognized standard works from Cairo to Cape Town
interop_step2_desc: Each country maintains its structure: wilaya, région, province, estado
interop_step3_desc: Facilitates trade in African regional economic communities
interop_step4_desc: UN, World Bank, DHL recognize Afroloc as valid address
apis_step1_desc: REST API with examples for African developers to easily integrate
apis_step2_desc: Flutterwave, Paystack, Yoco validate addresses in real-time

brand_guidelines_intro_text1: This manual establishes the official guidelines for the correct use of the AFROLOC visual identity. Compliance with these standards is essential to ensure consistency and brand recognition across all applications.
brand_guidelines_intro_text2: The AFROLOC brand represents Africa's digital addressing system, symbolizing inclusion, security and technological innovation for more than 1 billion Africans.

address_update_description: Fill in the fields below if your street has been given an official name or your house has received a number assigned by the local administration. The system will automatically update the address type from informal to formal.
address_type_explanation: After filling in street name AND number, the address will be automatically classified as Formal. Otherwise, it will be Informal. The address will only be Digital after certification by authorities.

createid_address_info_desc: Fill in the details of your location
createid_address_types_intro: The system automatically distinguishes between three types of addresses:
createid_type_formal_desc: Addresses with streets and numbers officially assigned by government administrations
createid_type_informal_desc: Addresses without an official street name or number assigned
createid_type_digital_desc: Type assigned after certification by authorities (replaces Formal/Informal)

createid_property_type_note_single: Houses, commercial properties and land only allow one registration per location

confwitness_confirmed_desc: Thank you for confirming your neighbour's address
confwitness_confirmed_body: Your confirmation helps build a reliable digital addressing system for Africa.
confwitness_subtitle: Enter the OTP code sent to your email
confwitness_contract_intro: I, the undersigned, hereby declare for all due purposes that:
confwitness_contract_item1: I personally know the resident whose address I am attesting;
confwitness_contract_item2: I confirm that the location information provided is true and correct;
confwitness_contract_item3: I am aware that this statement will be used for official address validation in the AFROLOC system;
confwitness_contract_item4: I understand that providing false information may result in legal consequences;
confwitness_contract_item5: I authorise the use of this declaration for identity and address verification purposes;
confwitness_contract_item6: I take full responsibility for the accuracy of the information provided.
confwitness_contract_footer: This declaration is governed by applicable law and constitutes a legal commitment to the accuracy of the information provided.
confwitness_agree_label: I have read and agree to the terms of the contract above
confwitness_placeholder_signature: Type your full name as signature
confwitness_signature_hint: By typing your name, you are digitally signing this contract
confwitness_otp_hint: Enter the 6-digit code you received by email
confwitness_responsibility_body: By confirming, you attest that you personally know the resident and that the address is correct.
confwitness_toast_resend_desc: A new code will be sent shortly

docslib_delete_confirm_prefix: Are you sure you want to delete

gpsval_notes_required: Please add notes about the validation

gpsval_select_to_add: Select an address to add GPS coordinates
gpsval_empty_state_hint: Select a formal address from the list to add and validate official GPS coordinates.
gpsval_official_validation_text: By submitting, you are officially certifying that these GPS coordinates correspond to the registered formal address.
gpsval_geolocation_unsupported: Geolocation not supported by the browser
gpsval_validation_notes_hint: (Describe the method and context of the validation)
gpsval_notes_placeholder: e.g. Coordinates obtained by official topographic survey on [date]. Verification carried out by the local administration's technical team.
gpsval_liability_text: By submitting this validation, I confirm that the GPS coordinates provided have been officially verified and accurately correspond to the registered formal address.

syssetup_step_countries_desc: Activate and configure the countries where the system will operate
syssetup_step_divisions_desc: Import the administrative divisions (provinces, territories, communes)
syssetup_step_admins_desc: Create administrators with full access to the system
syssetup_step_regional_desc: Assign staff to manage specific regions
syssetup_page_subtitle: Configure the system for multi-regional and multinational operation
syssetup_hier_alert_desc: The system operates on 5 authorization levels: Level 1 (Local) → Level 2 (Communal) → Level 3 (Territorial) → Level 4 (Provincial) → Level 5 (National). Each higher level supervises the lower levels within its jurisdiction.
syssetup_progress_desc: Follow these steps to configure the complete system
syssetup_delegation_desc: Each level can create and manage users of the level immediately below within its geographic jurisdiction. A Provincial Admin (L4) can create Territorial Admins (L3) in its province.
syssetup_workflow_card_desc: Follow this sequence for continental deployment
syssetup_wf1_desc: Activate the countries (Angola, Mozambique, etc.) and configure administrative-level labels (Province, Territory, Commune, Quartier)
syssetup_wf2_desc: Import via CSV all administrative divisions of each country (official data from the INE or government bodies)
syssetup_wf3_desc: Promote the first user to Global Admin (Level 5) with full access to the system
syssetup_wf4_desc: Create a National Admin (Level 5) for each country with jurisdiction over the entire national territory
syssetup_wf5_desc: National Admins create Provincial Admins (L4), who create Territorial (L3), who create Communal (L2), who create Local Agents (L1)
syssetup_wf6_desc: Configure phone numbers for SMS validation in each administrative division
about_hero_subtitle: Revolutionising digital addressing in Africa through inclusive technology, community validation and precise geo-referencing.
about_mission_desc: To provide every African citizen with a unique, verifiable and permanent digital address, promoting financial inclusion, access to essential services and the strengthening of continental identity.
about_vision_desc: To become the leading continental digital addressing system, connecting more than 1 billion Africans and serving as fundamental infrastructure for the continent's sustainable development.
about_values_subtitle: Fundamental principles that guide every decision and action
about_value_inclusion_desc: We believe every African deserves a verifiable digital address, regardless of their location or socio-economic situation.
about_value_security_desc: We protect users' identity and privacy through community validation and advanced encryption.
about_value_community_desc: The system is validated by the community itself, ensuring authenticity and strengthening social ties.
about_value_interoperability_desc: We are building a continental system that works across borders, connecting all of Africa.
about_timeline_2023q1_desc: Start of the development of the continental digital addressing concept for Africa
about_timeline_2023q2_desc: Development of the first prototype and testing with communities in Angola
about_timeline_2023q4_desc: Implementation of the witness validation system and authorisation levels
about_timeline_2024q1_desc: Launch in multiple African countries with multilingual support
about_timeline_2024q2_desc: Partnerships with financial institutions for banking inclusion
about_timeline_2024q3_desc: Launch of the open API for developers and companies
about_team_subtitle: Specialists dedicated to revolutionising digital addressing in Africa
about_team_ceo_desc: PhD in Geographic Information Systems, 15 years of experience in African digital infrastructure.
about_team_cto_desc: Former lead engineer at Google Maps, specialist in geo-referencing and distributed systems.
about_team_community_desc: Led digital inclusion projects in 12 African countries, fluent in 6 African languages.
about_team_engineering_desc: Systems architect with experience in large-scale platforms and blockchain.
about_cta_subtitle: Be part of the transformation that is changing how Africa connects

brand_toast_pdf_generated_desc: The Brand Book was downloaded successfully.
brand_color_primary_gold_usage: Main brand color, primary buttons, highlights
brand_value_security_desc: Data and digital identity protection with the highest standards.
brand_value_inclusion_desc: Accessible addressing for over 1 billion Africans.
brand_value_innovation_desc: Cutting-edge technology adapted to local realities.
brand_type_body: Paragraph text for content and descriptions.
brand_tone_professional_example: AFROLOC offers the definitive solution for digital addressing in Africa.
brand_tone_accessible_desc: Simple language everyone can understand, without unnecessary jargon.
brand_tone_accessible_example: Create your unique address in seconds, directly from your phone.
brand_tone_inspiring_desc: Communicate the vision of positive impact and transformation.
brand_tone_inspiring_example: Together, we are building the future of African digital infrastructure.
brand_tone_empathetic_desc: Demonstrate understanding of user needs and challenges.
brand_tone_empathetic_example: We know an address can change everything. That is why we simplified the process.
brand_dos_1: Symbol with text on the right (horizontal version)
brand_dos_2: Symbol alone with adequate protection space
brand_dos_3: Adequate contrast on light or dark backgrounds
brand_dos_4: Original proportions maintained in all applications
brand_dos_5: Official colors from the brand palette
brand_donts_4: Do not apply unauthorized effects (shadows, outlines)
brand_donts_5: Do not use on low contrast backgrounds
brand_donts_6: Do not add elements to the logo
brand_donts_7: Do not use low resolution versions
brand_digital_app_icon_spec: Symbol with golden gradient background (iOS: 1024px, Android: 512px)
brand_cover_tagline_1: Pioneering the future of African addressing through innovative digital solutions.
brand_cover_tagline_2: Building bridges between traditional methods and digital transformation.
brand_intro_desc: This Brand Book establishes the official guidelines for the correct use of the AFROLOC visual identity.
brand_mission_text: Democratizing access to secure and verifiable digital addresses for every African citizen, promoting financial, social and economic inclusion through innovative technology.
brand_vision_text: To be the leading digital addressing platform for the African continent, connecting over 1 billion people to opportunities through secure and verifiable location identities.
brand_values_subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do
brand_logo_subtitle: The Afroloc emblem represents location, security and African connection
brand_palette_subtitle: A sophisticated palette combining deep navy with rich golden accents
brand_typography_subtitle: A modern typographic system that balances authority with accessibility
brand_tone_subtitle: How we communicate with our audience
brand_usage_subtitle: Correct and incorrect uses of the brand
brand_digital_subtitle: How to apply the brand across digital touchpoints
brand_email_sig_tagline: AFROLOC - Digital Addressing System of Africa
brand_footer_note: Brand Guidelines v2.0 • For internal use and approved partners

addwitness_subtitle: Invite a neighbour to confirm your address
addwitness_process_intro: The regional validator will send an SMS to the witness with the options:
addwitness_process_outro: The witness must reply confirming or denying that you live at this address.
addwitness_card_desc: Enter the AFROLOC of a neighbour who can confirm your residence.
addwitness_card_desc_emphasis: The witness must have an active AFROLOC and a validated address.
addwitness_req_active_text: Must have a verified or certified AFROLOC
addwitness_req_validated_text: The witness must have their own address validated by an authority or other witnesses
addwitness_req_proximity_text: Must reside within 100 metres of your location
addwitness_req_sms_text: Will receive an SMS from the regional validator with YES/NO options
addwitness_req_minimum_text: You need 3 confirmed witnesses for validation
addwitness_req_restriction_text: You cannot add yourself as a witness
addwitness_toast_invalid_witness_desc: This AFROLOC does not exist. The witness must have a verified AFROLOC to participate.
addwitness_toast_not_active_desc: This AFROLOC is not active. The witness must have a verified or certified AFROLOC.
addwitness_toast_address_not_validated_d: This witness does not have a validated address. Approved authority or witness validation is required.
addwitness_toast_cannot_add_self_desc: You cannot add yourself as a witness.
addwitness_toast_duplicate_desc: This witness has already been added
addwitness_toast_notification_failed_des: Witness added but the notification could not be sent.
addwitness_toast_success_desc: Witness added successfully. They will receive a confirmation request via SMS.

authval_signature_hint: This will be recorded as the official validator signature
authval_requirement_1: You must be a verified authority (Level 2+)
authval_requirement_2: The identity must have completed community verification
authval_requirement_3: Your signature will be permanently recorded
authval_requirement_4: This action will change the identity status to "verified"

privacy_draft_notice: This document must be reviewed by legal counsel and adapted to the applicable data protection laws before being used in production.
privacy_s1_body: AFROLOC is operated by AFROFINTEK GmbH. This policy explains which data we collect, what we use it for and what rights you have over it.
privacy_s2_item1_body: name, mobile phone number and, where applicable, e-mail.
privacy_s2_item2_body: GPS coordinates and photo metadata (EXIF) used to create and verify your AFROLOC address.
privacy_s2_item3_body: witness confirmations and documents you upload.
privacy_s2_item4_body: technical logs for security and fraud prevention.
privacy_s3_body: To create and verify your digital address, prevent fraud, comply with legal obligations and improve the service. We do not sell your personal data.
privacy_s4_body: We share data only with providers necessary for operation (e.g. SMS delivery, maps) and with authorities when required by law, always to the minimum extent necessary.
privacy_s5_body: We apply technical and organisational measures to protect your data and retain it only for as long as necessary for the purposes described or as required by law.
privacy_s6_body: You can access, correct, export or request the deletion of your data, and withdraw consents. To exercise these rights, please contact us.

regionval_notifications_blocked_desc: Please enable notifications in your browser settings

regionval_empty_badge: Notifications will alert you to new requests

terms_draft_notice: This document must be reviewed by legal counsel before use in production.
terms_s1_body: By creating an account or using AFROLOC, you accept these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
terms_s2_body: AFROLOC assigns a unique, georeferenced and verifiable digital address. The address results from data you provide (location, photo) and from community/administrative validation.
terms_s3_body: You are responsible for keeping your access confidential and for the accuracy of the data you submit. False or fraudulent data may lead to suspension.
terms_s4_body: You may not use the service for unlawful purposes, falsify locations, circumvent verification mechanisms or harm third parties.
terms_s5_body: The software, the address-coding algorithms and the AFROLOC brand are the property of AFROFINTEK GmbH. Copying, reverse engineering or unauthorised use is prohibited.
terms_s6_body: The service is provided "as is". To the extent permitted by law, AFROFINTEK is not liable for indirect damages arising from its use.
terms_s7_body: We may update these Terms; we will notify you of relevant changes. These Terms are governed by the law applicable to the operator's registered office, without prejudice to mandatory consumer rights.
adminlogin_code_sent_desc: A verification code has been sent to your
adminlogin_security_notice: This login portal is for system administrators only. All access attempts are logged and monitored.

backupcodes_security_item_primary_method: Use backup codes only when you cannot access your primary 2FA method

backupcodes_not_shown_again: These codes will not be shown again. Make sure to save them in a secure location before leaving this page.
